passages.title: Cloze Test 17
passages.text: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has become an integral part of modern technology. (17) ______ its benefits, there are also concerns regarding job displacement. Experts argue that while AI might automate routine tasks, it also creates (18) ______ opportunities for those with high-tech skills. (19) ______, the transition to an AI-driven economy will require significant investment in education. Governments must ensure that the workforce is (20) ______ equipped to handle these changes. (21) ______ the long-term effects of AI remain uncertain, the immediate impact is undeniable.

passages.title: Cloze Test 22
passages.text: Marine ecosystems are essential for the health of our planet. They (22) ______ a vast array of life forms. Overfishing and pollution (23) ______ the balance of these delicate environments. (24) ______, climate change is causing ocean temperatures to rise, leading to coral bleaching. To protect our oceans, international cooperation is (25) ______. We must act now (26) ______ it is too late to reverse the damage.

questions.question: The sudden ______ of the global economy due to the pandemic caught many financial institutions off guard, necessitating immediate intervention.

options.A: curtailment
options.B: proliferation
options.C: subjugation
options.D: accumulation
options.E: manifestation

questions.question: In some ecosystems, specific plant species have evolved to thrive in ______ conditions where others would perish.

options.A: adversarial
options.B: haphazard
options.C: frivolous
options.D: tenuous
options.E: adverse

questions.question: The researcher was able to ______ the complex findings of the study into a concise report for the public.

options.A: intensify
options.B: distill
options.C: reconcile
options.D: undermine
options.E: neglect

questions.question: Many historians argue that the industrial revolution was not a sudden event but a process that occurred ______ over several decades.

options.A: sporadically
options.B: instantly
options.C: gradually
options.D: abruptly
options.E: precariously

questions.question: The treaty was designed to ______ the long-standing tensions between the two neighboring countries and promote peace.

options.A: exacerbate
options.B: allay
options.C: trigger
options.D: contravene
options.E: scrutinize

questions.question: Because the evidence provided was ______, the judge decided to dismiss the case due to lack of proof.

options.A: comprehensive
options.B: conclusive
options.C: substantial
options.D: scanty
options.E: noteworthy

questions.question: If the committee ______ the proposal earlier, the project ______ by now.

options.A: reviewed / would finish
options.B: had reviewed / would have been finished
options.C: reviews / will be finished
options.D: was reviewing / would have finished
options.E: has reviewed / will be finished

questions.question: The discovery of the ancient ruins ______ as one of the most significant archaeological finds of the century.

options.A: is being regarded
options.B: has been regarding
options.C: regards
options.D: is regarded
options.E: was regarding

questions.question: Not until the 19th century ______ the true nature of bacteria and its role in disease.

options.A: scientists understood
options.B: did scientists understand
options.C: scientists have understood
options.D: scientists were understanding
options.E: do scientists understand

questions.question: The professor suggested that the students ______ their research papers at least two weeks before the deadline.

options.A: submitted
options.B: submitting
options.C: submit
options.D: to submit
options.E: would submit

questions.question: Environmentalists are concerned ______ the impact ______ microplastics on marine life.

options.A: about / of
options.B: on / with
options.C: to / for
options.D: with / by
options.E: in / about

questions.question: Hardly ______ the plane touched the ground ______ the passengers began to unbuckle their seatbelts.

options.A: did / than
options.B: had / when
options.C: has / that
options.D: was / as
options.E: would / then

questions.question: The energy policy of the government is ______ ambitious ______ it requires a complete overhaul of the current infrastructure.

options.A: as / as
options.B: so / that
options.C: more / than
options.D: such / that
options.E: too / for

questions.question: The city council is considering building a new park, ______ will provide residents with more green space.

options.A: that
options.B: where
options.C: whose
options.D: which
options.E: whom

questions.question: Ancient civilizations used various methods to keep time, ______ of which were remarkably accurate for their era.

options.A: much
options.B: each
options.C: many
options.D: every
options.E: any

questions.question: The expansion of the Roman Empire ______ a profound impact on the legal systems of many modern nations.

options.A: had had
options.B: has had
options.C: was having
options.D: is having
options.E: will have had

questions.question: (Paragraph 1: The Impact of Artificial Intelligence) Artificial Intelligence (AI) has become an integral part of modern technology. (17) ______ its benefits, there are also concerns regarding job displacement.

options.A: Despite
options.B: Because of
options.C: As for
options.D: Regardless
options.E: Due to

questions.question: (Paragraph 1: Continued) Experts argue that while AI might automate routine tasks, it also creates (18) ______ opportunities for those with high-tech skills.

options.A: limited
options.B: negligible
options.C: new
options.D: obsolete
options.E: redundant

questions.question: (Paragraph 1: Continued) (19) ______, the transition to an AI-driven economy will require significant investment in education.

options.A: However
options.B: Therefore
options.C: In contrast
options.D: Similarly
options.E: For example

questions.question: (Paragraph 1: Continued) Governments must ensure that the workforce is (20) ______ equipped to handle these changes.

options.A: adequately
options.B: vaguely
options.C: scarcely
options.D: initially
options.E: roughly

questions.question: (Paragraph 1: Continued) (21) ______ the long-term effects of AI remain uncertain, the immediate impact is undeniable.

options.A: Since
options.B: While
options.C: Unless
options.D: Whether
options.E: Because

questions.question: (Paragraph 2: Marine Biodiversity) Marine ecosystems are essential for the health of our planet. They (22) ______ a vast array of life forms.

options.A: contain
options.B: destabilize
options.C: jeopardize
options.D: exclude
options.E: hinder

questions.question: (Paragraph 2: Continued) Overfishing and pollution (23) ______ the balance of these delicate environments.

options.A: threaten
options.B: improve
options.C: sustain
options.D: conserve
options.E: facilitate

questions.question: (Paragraph 2: Continued) (24) ______, climate change is causing ocean temperatures to rise, leading to coral bleaching.

options.A: Moreover
options.B: On the other hand
options.C: Conversely
options.D: Instead
options.E: Nevertheless

questions.question: (Paragraph 2: Continued) To protect our oceans, international cooperation is (25) ______.

options.A: imperative
options.B: optional
options.C: redundant
options.D: negligible
options.E: superficial

questions.question: (Paragraph 2: Continued) We must act now (26) ______ it is too late to reverse the damage.

options.A: before
options.B: after
options.C: until
options.D: since
options.E: when

questions.question: Although renewable energy sources are becoming more efficient, ______ .

options.A: they are still more expensive than fossil fuels in some regions
options.B: solar power is the most popular choice among homeowners
options.C: the government provides subsidies for wind farm projects
options.D: they have replaced coal entirely in many developed nations
options.E: environmental impact assessments are no longer necessary

questions.question: Unless the international community takes immediate action against carbon emissions, ______ .

options.A: global temperatures have stabilized in the last decade
options.B: the effects of global warming will become irreversible
options.C: scientists have developed new carbon capture technologies
options.D: public awareness of the issue is gradually increasing
options.E: countries will continue to ignore the Paris Agreement

questions.question: The suspect denied having any involvement in the robbery, ______ .

options.A: whereas the police found his fingerprints at the scene
options.B: so the judge decided to release him immediately
options.C: because he was busy working at the office at that time
options.D: even if he had no alibi for the night of the crime
options.E: since he was seen near the bank by several witnesses

questions.question: Many people prefer to live in urban areas ______ .

options.A: due to the lack of job opportunities in cities
options.B: so that they can avoid the noise and pollution
options.C: because cities offer better access to healthcare and education
options.D: despite the fact that rural life is more stressful
options.E: since the cost of living is much lower in metropolitan areas

questions.question: Hardly had the scientist published his groundbreaking paper ______ .

options.A: when he received a prestigious award for his work
options.B: after he had conducted several follow-up experiments
options.C: than other researchers began to challenge his findings
options.D: before he could celebrate his success with his colleagues
options.E: unless the results were verified by independent labs

questions.question: Since the introduction of digital banking, ______ .

options.A: traditional bank branches have become increasingly redundant
options.B: people used to carry large amounts of cash with them
options.C: cybersecurity is no longer a concern for financial institutions
options.D: the number of physical transactions has increased significantly
options.E: it has never been easier to lose track of one's spending

questions.question: No matter how hard the team practiced, ______ .

options.A: they were confident that they would win the championship
options.B: they could not overcome the opponent's superior strategy
options.C: the coach was satisfied with their performance in the match
options.D: every player felt that they had improved their skills
options.E: they managed to score several goals in the final minutes

questions.question: The bridge was closed to traffic ______ .

options.A: so that the engineers could inspect the structural damage
options.B: because it was the most important landmark in the city
options.C: in case the weather conditions improved during the day
options.D: since the traffic volume had decreased over the years
options.E: although the construction work had been completed on time

questions.question: While some scholars believe that the library was destroyed by fire, ______ .

options.A: others argue that it was gradually abandoned over time
options.B: it was once the largest repository of knowledge in the world
options.C: the ruins were discovered by a team of archaeologists
options.D: it contained thousands of ancient scrolls and manuscripts
options.E: the exact location of the library remains a mystery

questions.question: In light of the recent economic downturn, ______ .

options.A: the company has decided to expand its operations abroad
options.B: most consumers are spending more money on luxury items
options.C: the government is implementing new austerity measures
options.D: unemployment rates have reached an all-time low
options.E: interest rates are expected to rise significantly next year

questions.question: Quantum physics is a branch of science that deals with the behavior of matter and energy on an atomic and subatomic scale.

options.A: Kuantum fiziği, madde ve enerjinin atomik ve atom altı düzeydeki davranışıyla ilgilenen bir bilim dalıdır.
options.B: Atomik ve atom altı ölçekteki madde ve enerji hareketlerini inceleyen bilim dalına kuantum fiziği denir.
options.C: Madde ve enerjinin atom altı seviyedeki davranışlarını araştıran kuantum fiziği önemli bir bilim dalıdır.
options.D: Kuantum fiziği, atom ve atom altı ölçeklerdeki enerji ile madde arasındaki ilişkiyi inceler.
options.E: Atomik ve atom altı düzeyde madde ve enerjiyle ilgilenen bilim dalı kuantum fiziği olarak bilinir.

questions.question: Despite being one of the most visited cities in the world, Paris still manages to maintain its unique charm and historical atmosphere.

options.A: Dünyanın en çok ziyaret edilen şehirlerinden biri olmasına rağmen, Paris hala eşsiz cazibesini ve tarihi atmosferini korumayı başarmaktadır.
options.B: Paris, dünyanın en çok ziyaret edilen şehirlerinden biri olduğu halde tarihi atmosferini ve cazibesini yitirmemiştir.
options.C: Dünyanın en popüler şehirlerinden biri olan Paris, kendine has cazibesini ve tarihini her zaman korumuştur.
options.D: Paris hala eşsiz bir cazibeye ve tarihi atmosfere sahiptir çünkü dünyanın en çok ziyaret edilen şehridir.
options.E: Eşsiz cazibesi ve tarihi atmosferiyle Paris, dünyanın en çok ziyaret edilen şehirleri arasında yer almaktadır.

questions.question: The success of the mission depended not only on the technical skills of the astronauts but also on their ability to work as a team under pressure.

options.A: Görevin başarısı sadece astronotların teknik becerilerine değil, aynı zamanda baskı altında bir ekip olarak çalışma yeteneklerine de bağlıydı.
options.B: Astronotların teknik becerilerinin yanı sıra baskı altında takım çalışması yapmaları görevin başarısı için gerekliydi.
options.C: Görevin başarılı olması için astronotların hem teknik becerilere sahip olması hem de baskı altında iyi çalışmaları gerekiyordu.
options.D: Sadece teknik beceriler değil, astronotların baskı altındaki ekip çalışması da görevin başarısını belirledi.
options.E: Astronotlar baskı altında ekip olarak çalışabildikleri ve teknik becerileri yüksek olduğu için görev başarılı oldu.

questions.question: Son yıllarda yapılan araştırmalar, düzenli egzersizin sadece fiziksel sağlığı değil, aynı zamanda zihinsel fonksiyonları da iyileştirdiğini göstermektedir.

options.A: Recent research shows that regular exercise improves not only physical health but also mental functions.
options.B: Studies conducted in recent years suggest that physical health and mental functions are improved by exercise.
options.C: Regular exercise has been shown by recent studies to be beneficial for both physical and mental health.
options.D: According to recent research, regular exercise is essential for improving physical health and mental functions.
options.E: Research in recent years indicates that regular exercise improves mental functions as well as physical health.

questions.question: Yenilenebilir enerji kaynaklarına yatırım yapmak, fosil yakıtlara olan bağımlılığımızı azaltmak için hayati öneme sahiptir.

options.A: Investing in renewable energy sources is vital to reduce our dependence on fossil fuels.
options.B: To reduce dependency on fossil fuels, we must invest in renewable energy sources immediately.
options.C: Renewable energy investments are necessary if we want to decrease our reliance on fossil fuels.
options.D: It is vital to invest in renewable energy sources so that fossil fuel dependence can be reduced.
options.E: The dependence on fossil fuels can only be reduced by investing in renewable energy sources.

questions.question: Yapay zeka teknolojisi geliştikçe, etik kuralların ve yasal düzenlemelerin önemi her geçen gün daha da artmaktadır.

options.A: As artificial intelligence technology develops, the importance of ethical rules and legal regulations is increasing day by day.
options.B: The development of AI technology makes ethical rules and legal regulations more important every day.
options.C: Ethical rules and legal regulations are becoming more important as AI technology continues to evolve.
options.D: With the growth of AI technology, the significance of ethics and laws is increasing daily.
options.E: The importance of ethical rules and legal regulations increases every day alongside AI technology developments.

questions.question: According to the passage, what is the main obstacle to the formal classification of the Anthropocene?

options.A: The lack of consensus on a specific physical marker in the geological record
options.B: The refusal of the scientific community to acknowledge human impact on Earth
options.C: The difficulty in measuring the surge in plastic consumption since the 1950s
options.D: The political pressure from industrial nations against the term
options.E: The disagreement between Paul Crutzen and other stratigraphers

questions.question: The term 'Great Acceleration' is used in the text to describe...

options.A: The rapid expansion of industrial activities during the 18th century
options.B: A period in the mid-20th century marked by significant environmental and technological shifts
options.C: The geological process of sedimentary rock formation
options.D: The speed at which stratigraphers are working to ratify the new epoch
options.E: The increasing rate of global population growth in the 19th century

questions.question: It can be inferred from the passage that the term 'Anthropocene'...

options.A: Is officially recognized by all international geological societies
options.B: Was first coined in the mid-20th century following nuclear tests
options.C: Is more widely accepted as a conceptual tool than as a formal geological epoch
options.D: Primarily focuses on the positive impacts of human innovation
options.E: Has been proven false by recent stratigraphic findings

questions.question: What is the author's attitude towards the concept of the Anthropocene?

options.A: Dismissive of its scientific validity
options.B: Highly critical of the 'golden spike' methodology
options.C: Analytical regarding its academic status and utility
options.D: Skeptical about the extent of human-induced change
options.E: Indifferent to the debate among stratigraphers

questions.question: Which of the following is true about neuroplasticity according to the passage?

options.A: It is a process that only occurs in the early stages of human development
options.B: It allows the brain to adapt to new information and recover from trauma
options.C: It is an irreversible process that leads to neurodegenerative diseases
options.D: It remains constant in its intensity throughout a person's life
options.E: It is only triggered by external physical injuries to the skull

questions.question: The word 'malleable' in the passage is closest in meaning to...

options.A: Rigid
options.B: Adaptable
options.C: Fragile
options.D: Obsolete
options.E: Complex

questions.question: How does the passage contrast the plasticity of adult brains with that of children?

options.A: Adults have more neural connections but less ability to change them
options.B: Children's brains are less responsive to learning than adult brains
options.C: Adult plasticity is possible but typically requires more effort or stimuli
options.D: There is no difference in the rate of plasticity between the two groups
options.E: Adults cannot form new neural connections after a certain age

questions.question: What is one clinical application of neuroplasticity mentioned in the text?

options.A: The prevention of all aging-related cognitive decline
options.B: The development of vaccines for neurodegenerative diseases
options.C: The rehabilitation of individuals who have suffered a stroke
options.D: The enhancement of memory in healthy young adults
options.E: The surgical replacement of damaged brain regions

questions.question: The passage emphasizes that the Silk Road was...

options.A: The only way for Europeans to reach Asia in the 14th century
options.B: A multifaceted network facilitating both material and cultural exchange
options.C: Primarily responsible for the total collapse of Eastern economies
options.D: A single, well-paved road stretching from Rome to Beijing
options.E: Exclusively used for the transport of high-value silk products

questions.question: According to the passage, how did Buddhism reach China?

options.A: Through the efforts of European explorers during the Age of Discovery
options.B: By sea routes established by the Ottoman Empire
options.C: Via the trade networks that comprised the Silk Road
options.D: As a result of the migration caused by the Black Death
options.E: Through the introduction of papermaking technology

questions.question: Which of the following was a negative consequence of the Silk Road?

options.A: The loss of traditional Chinese papermaking techniques
options.B: The introduction of gunpowder to the Middle East
options.C: The transmission of the bubonic plague across continents
options.D: The decline of maritime trade in the Indian Ocean
options.E: The suppression of religious diversity in India

questions.question: Why did the Silk Road's importance eventually diminish?

options.A: Because silk became a common and inexpensive commodity
options.B: Due to the total eradication of Buddhism in the East
options.C: Because of new maritime routes and changes in political control
options.D: As a result of the discovery of gold in the Americas
options.E: Because the land routes became too dangerous due to the Black Death

questions.question: What did Albert Einstein find troubling about quantum entanglement?

options.A: It required too much energy to maintain in a lab
options.B: It appeared to violate the speed-of-light limit of classical physics
options.C: It was only observable in very small particles
options.D: It had no practical applications in the field of computing
options.E: It was based on purely theoretical calculations with no evidence

questions.question: According to the text, 'qubits' are essential because they...

options.A: Prevent environmental interference from affecting computers
options.B: Travel faster than the speed of light across long distances
options.C: Enable significantly higher processing speeds in computers
options.D: Are the only particles that can be entangled in a vacuum
options.E: Eliminate the need for quantum cryptography

questions.question: The term 'coherence' in the context of the passage refers to...

options.A: The ability of a scientist to explain complex theories
options.B: The stability of the link between entangled particles
options.C: The speed at which a quantum computer operates
options.D: The distance between two linked particles
options.E: The process of turning classical data into quantum data

questions.question: What is the main challenge in utilizing quantum entanglement for technology?

options.A: The high cost of producing entangled particles
options.B: The lack of mathematical models to describe the phenomenon
options.C: The sensitivity of entangled states to the surrounding environment
options.D: The refusal of governments to fund quantum research
options.E: The biological risks associated with quantum radiation

questions.question: How does behavioral economics differ from traditional economic theory?

options.A: It focuses exclusively on the behavior of large corporations
options.B: It assumes that humans are always rational and self-interested
options.C: It acknowledges that cognitive biases affect economic decisions
options.D: It argues that the 'Homo Economicus' model is the only valid one
options.E: It rejects the idea that policymakers should influence public choice

questions.question: The concept of 'loss aversion' suggests that...

options.A: People are more motivated to prevent a loss than to achieve a gain
options.B: Losing money is a necessary part of a healthy economy
options.C: Individuals always make decisions based on mathematical probability
options.D: Gaining a reward is twice as satisfying as losing the same amount
options.E: People tend to forget their losses more quickly than their gains

questions.question: In the context of the passage, what is a 'nudge'?

options.A: A strict regulation that forces people to behave in a certain way
options.B: A financial penalty for making poor economic choices
options.C: A subtle method to influence behavior toward a positive outcome
options.D: A marketing strategy used by companies to increase debt
options.E: A psychological disorder caused by over-reliance on heuristics

questions.question: The example of the 'opt-out' system for organ donation is used to illustrate...

options.A: The failure of behavioral economics in public policy
options.B: The effectiveness of nudges in changing societal outcomes
options.C: The inherent selfishness of most individuals in a society
options.D: The high cost of implementing behavioral interventions
options.E: The complexity of legal systems in modern democracies

questions.question:
Professor: Have you had a chance to look at the feedback on your thesis proposal yet?
Student: Yes, I have. You suggested that my scope is far too broad for a master's level project.
Professor: ----
Student: I see. So, instead of looking at the entire 19th-century literature, I should focus on a specific decade or a single author?

options.A: Exactly. You need to narrow it down to a manageable set of variables.
options.B: I actually think you should add more chapters to cover the history.
options.C: Don't worry about the scope; the more data you have, the better.
options.D: I was impressed by how comprehensive your research plan was.
options.E: I think your thesis is already too short to be considered academic.

questions.question:
Doctor: Your blood tests show that your vitamin D levels are still quite low despite the supplements.
Patient: That's strange. I've been taking them every morning with my coffee.
Doctor: ----
Patient: Oh, I didn't know that. I'll start taking them with my main meal from now on.

options.A: Coffee actually increases the absorption of most vitamins.
options.B: You should probably double the dose if you aren't seeing results.
options.C: Vitamin D is fat-soluble, so it's best absorbed when taken with a meal containing fat.
options.D: It might be because the supplements you are using have expired.
options.E: Maybe you should spend more time indoors to avoid sun exposure.

questions.question:
Interviewer: Your resume is impressive, but we are looking for someone with more experience in project management.
Candidate: I understand. However, I have led several volunteer teams in high-pressure environments.
Interviewer: ----
Candidate: Certainly. Last year, I organized a regional charity event that involved coordinating over fifty volunteers and three different vendors.

options.A: We are not really interested in your volunteer work at this stage.
options.B: Can you give me a specific example of a time you handled a difficult situation?
options.C: Project management is mostly about technical skills rather than leadership.
options.D: I think you are overqualified for the position we are offering.
options.E: How much salary are you expecting for a role like this?

questions.question:
Journalist: Do you believe that the new environmental regulations will actually help reduce carbon emissions?
Politician: Absolutely. We've set very clear targets for the industrial sector.
Journalist: ----
Politician: We have introduced a series of heavy fines and a mandatory reporting system to ensure compliance.

options.A: But how do you plan to enforce these rules effectively?
options.B: Most people think the regulations are already too strict.
options.C: Why did you decide to ignore the advice of the scientific community?
options.D: Are you planning to increase the taxes on electric vehicles too?
options.E: Will these regulations be applied to the agricultural sector as well?

questions.question:
Librarian: I'm afraid this book is two weeks overdue, so there will be a small fine.
Student: I'm so sorry. I thought the loan period was for a month.
Librarian: ----
Student: Okay, I'll make sure to check the date on the website next time.

options.A: It's fine, you can keep the book for another week for free.
options.B: The policy changed recently; it's now only two weeks for new releases.
options.C: I can waive the fine if you promise to donate a new book.
options.D: Are you sure you didn't lose the receipt we gave you?
options.E: We don't accept cash payments for fines at this counter.

questions.question: The hypothesis that the virus originated in a laboratory has been largely dismissed by the scientific community due to a lack of empirical evidence.

options.A: Scientists believe the virus came from a lab despite having very little evidence.
options.B: The lack of scientific proof has led most researchers to reject the idea that the virus was lab-created.
options.C: The scientific community is still divided on whether the virus was created in a laboratory or not.
options.D: Because there is no evidence, scientists are trying to prove the virus did not come from a lab.
options.E: Empirical evidence suggests that the virus could not have originated anywhere except in a laboratory.

questions.question: Hardly had the new law been implemented when several small businesses began to complain about its financial burden.

options.A: Small businesses complained so much that the new law could not be implemented.
options.B: As soon as the new law went into effect, various small companies started expressing concerns about the costs.
options.C: The new law was implemented only after small businesses stopped complaining about the financial burden.
options.D: Even though the law was implemented, small businesses did not feel any financial pressure.
options.E: The financial burden on small businesses was the main reason why the new law was implemented.

questions.question: Had the government invested more in renewable energy a decade ago, the current energy crisis might not have been so severe.

options.A: The government invested in renewable energy ten years ago, yet the energy crisis is still very bad.
options.B: The current energy crisis is severe because the government refuses to invest in renewable energy.
options.C: If the government had put more money into green energy ten years ago, today's energy problems could have been less serious.
options.D: The energy crisis would be even worse today if the government hadn't invested in renewables a decade ago.
options.E: A decade ago, the government predicted that the energy crisis would be severe regardless of investments.

questions.question: It is not so much his lack of talent as his lack of discipline that prevents him from succeeding in the music industry.

options.A: He is talented but undisciplined, and this is what stops him from being successful in music.
options.B: He lacks both talent and discipline, which makes it impossible for him to succeed in music.
options.C: His lack of talent is the primary reason why he hasn't achieved success in the music industry.
options.D: If he were more talented, his lack of discipline wouldn't be such a big problem for his career.
options.E: The music industry is so competitive that neither talent nor discipline can guarantee success.

questions.question: The concept of 'sustainable development' first gained international prominence in the 1987 Brundtland Report. It defined the term as development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs. ----. This requires a delicate balance between economic growth, environmental protection, and social equity.

options.A: Therefore, the report focused exclusively on the industrial output of developed nations.
options.B: In other words, it suggests that we must live within the limits of our planet's natural resources.
options.C: However, many critics argue that the term is too vague to be useful in practical policy.
options.D: The report also predicted that global temperatures would rise by five degrees by 2050.
options.E: Sustainable development is no longer a priority for most modern governments.

questions.question: In the field of linguistics, the 'Critical Period Hypothesis' suggests that there is a specific window of time in early childhood during which language acquisition is most effortless. ----. After this window closes, learning a first or second language becomes significantly more difficult and often results in a lack of native-like fluency.

options.A: This theory was first proposed by Eric Lenneberg in 1967.
options.B: Children are usually able to learn multiple languages at the same time.
options.C: Most linguists now believe that adults are actually better at learning grammar.
options.D: Language acquisition is a lifelong process that never truly ends.
options.E: This is why older adults are often more motivated to learn new languages.

questions.question: The Great Depression of the 1930s was the most severe economic downturn in the history of the industrialized world. It began after the stock market crash of October 1929, which sent Wall Street into a panic and wiped out millions of investors. ----. Over the next several years, consumer spending and investment dropped, causing steep declines in industrial output and rising levels of unemployment.

options.A: The recovery was rapid thanks to the immediate intervention of the Federal Reserve.
options.B: This event triggered a global chain reaction of bank failures and trade contractions.
options.C: Despite the crash, the agricultural sector remained surprisingly stable.
options.D: Most economists agree that the crash had no real impact on the average citizen.
options.E: The stock market actually regained all its losses within a few months.

questions.question: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has the potential to revolutionize healthcare by improving diagnostic accuracy and personalizing treatment plans. For instance, AI algorithms can analyze medical images with a precision that sometimes surpasses human radiologists. ----. Issues such as data privacy, algorithmic bias, and the loss of the human touch in patient care must be carefully managed.

options.A: Consequently, doctors will soon be completely replaced by autonomous machines.
options.B: However, the integration of AI also raises significant ethical and practical concerns.
options.C: AI systems are also being used to automate administrative tasks in hospitals.
options.D: Many patients prefer interacting with AI rather than human doctors.
options.E: The cost of implementing AI in healthcare is decreasing every year.

questions.question: (I) Dark matter is a hypothetical form of matter that is thought to account for approximately 85% of the matter in the universe. (II) Unlike normal matter, it does not emit, absorb, or reflect light, making it extremely difficult to detect directly. (III) Astronomers have observed that the sun is composed primarily of hydrogen and helium. (IV) Its presence is inferred from gravitational effects on visible matter and the structure of the universe. (V) Without dark matter, galaxies would likely fly apart rather than rotate in the way they do.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) The Renaissance was a period of intense cultural, artistic, and scientific rebirth in Europe. (II) It began in 14th-century Italy and eventually spread across the continent over the next few centuries. (III) Figures like Leonardo da Vinci and Michelangelo redefined the limits of human creativity. (IV) Modern digital art uses complex software to create hyper-realistic images. (V) This era also saw the rise of humanism, which emphasized the value and agency of human beings.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants and some other organisms use sunlight to synthesize foods. (II) It generally involves the green pigment chlorophyll and generates oxygen as a byproduct. (III) Carbon dioxide is absorbed from the atmosphere during this cycle. (IV) Global warming is caused by the excessive buildup of greenhouse gases like methane. (V) The energy stored in these sugar molecules fuels the growth and reproduction of the plant.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Social media platforms have fundamentally changed the way people communicate and consume information. (II) While they allow for instant global connectivity, they also contribute to the spread of misinformation. (III) Many users report feeling a sense of anxiety or 'FOMO' when browsing their feeds. (IV) Printing presses in the 15th century also revolutionized the way information was shared. (V) Furthermore, the algorithms used by these sites often create echo chambers that polarize public opinion.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) The Roman Empire was one of the most powerful and influential civilizations in history. (II) Its legal system, architecture, and language provided the foundation for much of Western society. (III) The Colosseum remains a testament to the engineering brilliance of Roman builders. (IV) Roman citizens often enjoyed public baths and chariot races as forms of entertainment. (V) The decline of the Mayan civilization in Central America remains a mystery to many archaeologists.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V
